title: Fern and Dewdrop Save the Stream

pageTimings.text:
The Crystal Stream was not singing.
Fern heard it first.
Or rather — she heard nothing.
She went to find Dewdrop.

"It flows," said Fern.
"But it does not sing," said Dewdrop.
"Something is wrong at the source."

"The song comes from the source," said Dewdrop.
"We must follow the stream up."
Fern looked at the deep forest ahead.
"Then we go," said Fern.

They followed the stream upstream.
The forest grew older.
The roots grew bigger.
The stream grew quieter.

At the source, they found it.
A great boulder had fallen.
It lay across the singing stones.
The stones could not sing.

Dewdrop tried to push it with water.
She pushed as hard as she could.
The boulder did not move.
"I am not strong enough," said Dewdrop.

Fern tried to push with her hands.
The boulder did not move.
Fern stepped back and thought.
"We need each other," said Fern.

Fern grew a strong vine around the boulder.
Dewdrop sent water under it to loosen the mud.
"Ready?" said Fern.
"Ready," said Dewdrop.

They pushed and pulled together.
The boulder shifted.
The mud let go.
The boulder rolled aside.

The water rushed over the singing stones.
And the Crystal Stream began to sing.
First very quietly.
Then louder.
Then loud enough to hear all the way to the Hollow Oak.

They sat on the bank and listened.
The stream sang around them.
"We did it," said Fern.
Dewdrop smiled.
"It sang itself. We just moved the stone."

At the Hollow Oak, everyone heard it.
"The stream is singing again!" said Pip.
"We just moved a stone," said Fern.
"And asked the water nicely," said Dewdrop.
"It did the rest."
